title: Open-Structured Return — Common Fields
description: Reference documentation for fields shared by every OSR (Researcher, Skill Writer, Eval Designer, Runner, Grader, Investigator). This file is NOT used directly by the validator; each osr-<agent>.schema.json inlines these fields so the validator can be implemented without $ref resolution. Changes here MUST be propagated by hand.
$comment: The fields below describe the minimum OSR contract. Agent-specific schemas extend with additional required fields, and both tolerate extras (additionalProperties: true) as open-world escape hatch.

status.description: success = work done + OSR complete. partial = work done but some fields omitted. failed = work NOT done. schema_insufficient = work done but schema cannot represent key findings; MUST accompany requested_schema_extension.

agent_type.description: Identifies which OSR schema to use. Hook uses this to pick the right validator.

cwd.description: Working directory the agent ran in. Must be an isolated path for worktree/tmpdir isolation checks.

tool_use_count_self_report.description: Agent's best-effort self-report. NOT authoritative — authoritative counts come from subagent_log.py.

surprises.description: Short signals about things the agent found worth flagging that aren't in the required fields. Keep 0-5 items. Each must include a suggested_action so the Orchestrator can decide without reading prose.

anomalies.description: Factual observations of things that contradict expectations. Typically triggered by the hook system (e.g., tool_count_self_report != tool_count_from_log).

meta_observations.description: Feedback about the distillation workflow itself (not about the repo being distilled). Used to evolve the process.

notes_path.description: Optional path to a free-form Markdown file in workspace/notes/. Used for long observations that don't fit the structured fields. Orchestrator reads this only in rich context_mode or when following up on a surprise.

notes_topic_tags.description: Short tags describing what's in notes_path, so Orchestrator can decide whether to open it.

requested_schema_extension.description: REQUIRED when status == schema_insufficient. Agent proposes a schema extension.

extras.description: Open-world pocket for fields not yet formalized. Orchestrator preserves but does not semantically consume these; they feed schema evolution meta-analysis.
